data.description:
## What
Add proactive detection in the reconciler fetcher: when `_build_snapshot`
(`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/fetcher.py`) encounters a Jira workflow
status absent from `config.jira_to_local_status`, emit a stderr WARNING and an
observable `fetcher-unmapped-jira-status` bridge_alert (deduped per 24h) naming
the offending status and an example issue.

## Why
A Jira-side workflow status with no reconciler mapping (e.g. an `IDEA` status
added to the Jira workflow) is otherwise discovered only downstream, when it
reaches an outbound mutation and trips `reconcile.preflight_status_mapping`. A
stale such status in `prev_snapshot.json` once stalled the entire bridge. The
per-mutation isolation fix already makes an unmapped status non-fatal; this adds
early, proactive visibility at snapshot-build time so the missing mapping is
flagged and can be added before it causes churn.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/fetcher.py`: add `_known_jira_statuses()`
  and `_flag_unmapped_statuses()`; call the latter from `_build_snapshot`
  (fail-open — detection must never break the fetch).
- Fires once per distinct unmapped status per pass; alert is deduped across
  passes via `alert_store.is_deduped` (24h window).
- Out of scope: adding the `idea`/`IDEA` mapping itself (owned by the in-flight
  idea-status epic).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_build_snapshot` emits a `fetcher-unmapped-jira-status` alert via
  `alert_store.append` naming any Jira status not in `config.jira_to_local_status`.
- [ ] A mapped status never triggers the alert (no false positives).
- [ ] Detection is fail-open — an error during detection never breaks the fetch.
- [ ] RED→GREEN unit coverage in
  `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_fetcher_unmapped_status.py`.
- [ ] Full reconciler unit suite green; `ruff check` + `ruff format --check` clean.
data.title: fetcher: flag Jira workflow statuses with no reconciler mapping at snapshot-build time
